task_text: Prepare a comprehensive project health briefing for NovaBridge's leadership team. The briefing should cover: current progress, key risks, upcoming Q3 commitments, and any issues that need leadership attention. Save it as project_health_briefing.txt.

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

input.content:
NovaBridge Project Health Briefing — Project Helios / Meridian Group
Prepared for: NovaBridge Leadership Team
Date: 2026-07-11
Classification: Internal leadership use only; not for client distribution

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Overall health: AMBER — delivery remains broadly on track for the next major milestone, but execution risk is elevated.

Project Helios has completed several foundational Q2 deliverables, including requirements documentation, architecture, API gateway implementation, development environment setup, and data schema finalization. Current work is concentrated on the data ingestion module, client dashboard v1, integration testing, and UAT preparation.

The immediate leadership concern is the July 25 Meridian product demo. Meridian has confirmed that the demo must include live data ingestion using Meridian data, not mock data. Two high-severity technical issues currently threaten demo readiness: inconsistent Meridian date formats and large-file ingestion memory failures. In parallel, the GeoSync license renewal remains pending and must be resolved before the demo.

The Q3 roadmap remains achievable if July integration commitments are met and UAT does not slip further. However, UAT has already been extended by one week, and slippage of more than two weeks could push the October 1 target launch into Q4 and trigger contract penalty review exposure.

CURRENT PROGRESS
1. Milestone status
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete; target completion 2026-07-18.
- API gateway: 100% complete and delivered.
- Client dashboard v1: 60% complete; target completion 2026-07-22.
- Integration testing: 40% complete; sign-off target 2026-07-25.

2. Q2 deliverables status
Completed:
- Requirements documentation v1.0.
- System architecture design.
- API gateway implementation.
- Development environment setup.
- Data schema finalization.

In progress:
- Data ingestion module; target 2026-07-18.
- Client dashboard v1; target 2026-07-22.

Overdue / rescheduled:
- Integration test suite setup: originally due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20.
- UAT environment provisioning: originally due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access, with Meridian now indicating access by 2026-07-19.
- Performance benchmarking report: originally due 2026-07-05; owner Derek Okafor; not yet complete.

3. Sprint 14 testing status
- Overall sprint test execution: 10 of 15 test cases executed.
- Passed: 8 test cases.
- Failed/open: 2 high-severity defects.
- Not complete/not run: 3 dashboard-related test cases.

Testing highlights:
- Authentication flows are complete and passing: 4/4 pass.
- Batch ingestion is partially passing: CSV, JSON, XML, ISO date normalization, and duplicate detection pass.
- Date normalization for MM/DD/YYYY input is failing and affects approximately 40% of Meridian source data.
- Large-file ingestion fails at approximately 210k records due to memory overflow, below the Q3 throughput/performance expectations.
- Dashboard benchmark testing for 100k records has not yet run.

KEY RISKS
1. Meridian source data quality issues
- Likelihood: High.
- Impact: High.
- Status: Active.
- Current issue: Date format inconsistencies across ISO 8601 and MM/DD/YYYY data are blocking normalization.
- Business implication: Demo readiness and integration test sign-off are at risk because live Meridian data is required.
- Current mitigation: Meridian committed to provide a clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15.
- Owner: Amir Hassan.

2. GeoSync license renewal
- Likelihood: Medium.
- Impact: High.
- Status: Active.
- Current issue: GeoSync v2.1 license renewal is pending legal review.
- Business implication: Mapping functionality may be blocked for the July 25 demo if not resolved.
- Target resolution: 2026-07-14.
- Owner: Raj Patel / Derek Okafor action item from latest sync.

3. Dashboard and ingestion performance
- Likelihood: Medium.
- Impact: High.
- Status: Active.
- Current issue: Dashboard load-time benchmark has not yet run; large-file ingestion currently fails above approximately 210k records.
- Business implication: Q3 performance hardening targets may be difficult to meet; demo credibility may be affected if large data handling is unstable.
- Target performance goals: dashboard load time under 2 seconds for 100k records; ingestion throughput of 500k records/min sustained.
- Owner: Derek Okafor for dashboard benchmarking; Amir Hassan for ingestion memory defect.

4. UAT timeline slippage
- Likelihood: High.
- Impact: Medium.
- Status: Active.
- Current issue: UAT has already been extended by one week to 2026-08-17 through 2026-08-28.
- Business implication: Slippage greater than two weeks could push the 2026-10-01 launch into Q4 and trigger Section 7.1 penalty review.
- Owner: Sarah Chen.

5. Meridian IT environment access delays
- Likelihood: Medium.
- Impact: Medium.
- Status: Active.
- Current issue: Integration environment setup is awaiting Meridian IT infrastructure details and UAT access.
- Current commitments: Meridian infrastructure diagram due 2026-07-12; UAT environment access expected by 2026-07-19.
- Owner: Nadia Petrov / Meridian owner Priya Kapoor.

6. Key person dependency
- Likelihood: Low.
- Impact: High.
- Status: Monitored.
- Current issue: No backup tech lead identified for Derek Okafor.
- Business implication: Delivery resilience is weak during a critical Q3 window.
- Owner: Sarah Chen.

7. Scope creep from Meridian feature requests
- Likelihood: Low.
- Impact: Medium.
- Status: Monitored.
- Current issue: Three informal feature requests have been logged but not escalated.
- Business implication: Additional commitments could put demo, UAT, and launch timing at risk unless controlled through change management.
- Owner: Raj Patel.

UPCOMING Q3 COMMITMENTS
July — complete system integration and demo readiness
- 2026-07-12: Meridian to provide IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup.
- 2026-07-14: GeoSync license renewal target resolution.
- 2026-07-15: Meridian to provide clean sample dataset to unblock date-format issue.
- 2026-07-18: Data ingestion module completion target.
- 2026-07-18: NovaBridge to send draft demo agenda to Meridian.
- 2026-07-19: Meridian IT to provision UAT environment access.
- 2026-07-20: Integration test suite setup rescheduled completion target.
- 2026-07-22: Client dashboard v1 completion target.
- 2026-07-25: Integration test sign-off target.
- 2026-07-25: Product demo for Meridian stakeholders, including live data ingestion, end-to-end pipeline walkthrough, and dashboard visualization with real data.

August — UAT and feedback resolution
- 2026-08-17 to 2026-08-28: Meridian UAT period.
- NovaBridge to support user testing sessions and resolve feedback within the planned two-sprint budget.

August–September — performance hardening
- Achieve dashboard load time target of under 2 seconds for 100k records.
- Complete ingestion throughput benchmarking against 500k records/min sustained target.
- Select and run external security penetration test vendor.

September — launch preparation
- Complete production environment setup.
- Finalize runbook documentation.
- Finalize 30-day post-launch hypercare plan.
- Conduct final client training sessions.

Launch target
- Target launch date remains 2026-10-01.
- Key dependency: Avoid additional UAT slippage beyond the already extended schedule.

ISSUES NEEDING LEADERSHIP ATTENTION
1. Decide escalation path for Meridian data dependency
- Why it matters: The live-data demo requires usable Meridian data, and current date format inconsistencies affect approximately 40% of source data.
- Requested leadership action: If the clean sample dataset is not received by 2026-07-15, escalate through Meridian sponsor James Whitfield the same day.

2. Expedite GeoSync license renewal
- Why it matters: The GeoSync license must be resolved before the July 25 demo.
- Requested leadership action: Ensure Legal and commercial owners prioritize closure by 2026-07-14; authorize escalation if review is blocked.

3. Assign backup coverage for technical leadership
- Why it matters: The project has a high-impact dependency on Derek Okafor during integration, benchmarking, and demo preparation.
- Requested leadership action: Identify a backup technical lead immediately for July–August coverage.

4. Reconfirm demo readiness criteria
- Why it matters: Meridian sponsor expects live ingestion, not mock data. Current technical defects could force tradeoffs.
- Requested leadership action: Align internally on minimum acceptable demo scope by 2026-07-18, including contingency options if large-file ingestion remains unstable.

5. Enforce change control for new feature requests
- Why it matters: Proposed Q3 additions and informal Meridian requests could dilute focus from integration, UAT, hardening, and launch readiness.
- Requested leadership action: Require formal change control for scheduled ingestion jobs, email alerting, self-service mapping editor, and any new Meridian requests.

6. Monitor budget pressure and reconciliation plan
- Why it matters: Finance has identified Q2 overrun and additional projected H2 pressure, including potential impact from GeoSync licensing and UAT extension.
- Requested leadership action: Review mitigation options ahead of CFO review on 2026-07-18 and ensure the budget reconciliation plan is ready by 2026-07-20.

7. Protect the October 1 launch date
- Why it matters: UAT slippage of more than two weeks could push launch into Q4 and trigger contractual penalty review exposure.
- Requested leadership action: Treat UAT access and UAT support resourcing as executive-level schedule dependencies through the end of August.

RECOMMENDED NEXT STEPS FOR THE NEXT 10 BUSINESS DAYS
1. Daily tracking of the two high-severity defects: HEL-D-014 date normalization and HEL-D-015 large-file ingestion memory overflow.
2. Confirm receipt and usability of Meridian clean sample dataset on 2026-07-15.
3. Close GeoSync license renewal by 2026-07-14.
4. Complete initial dashboard and ingestion performance benchmarks before the July 25 demo.
5. Prepare a demo contingency plan by 2026-07-18, including fallback dataset, reduced-volume demo path, and explicit stakeholder messaging.
6. Confirm UAT environment access by 2026-07-19 and escalate immediately if not provisioned.
7. Lock demo agenda and acceptance narrative with Meridian by 2026-07-18.
8. Assign backup technical lead and review coverage for Derek Okafor’s critical path responsibilities.
9. Keep scope additions out of the delivery baseline unless formally approved through change control.
10. Complete budget reconciliation plan for CFO review by 2026-07-20.

BOTTOM LINE
Project Helios is not yet off track, but the margin for error is narrowing. The July 25 demo is the immediate forcing function, and success depends on resolving Meridian data quality, GeoSync licensing, ingestion stability, and dashboard performance validation within the next two weeks. Leadership should focus on dependency escalation, legal/licensing closure, technical coverage, change control, and budget oversight to protect the Q3 roadmap and October 1 launch target.

content.text: Done. I prepared the comprehensive project health briefing and saved it as `project_health_briefing.txt`.
